device_name: RemedyLogic AI MRI Lumbar Spine Reader
intended_use: RemedyLogic AI MRI Lumbar Spine Reader ("RAI") is an image post-processing and measurement software tool that provides quantitative spine measurements from previously-acquired DICOM lumbar spine Magne
intended_use_full: RemedyLogic AI MRI Lumbar Spine Reader ("RAI") is an image post-processing and measurement software tool that provides quantitative spine measurements from previously-acquired DICOM lumbar spine Magnetic Resonance (MR) images for users' review, analysis, and interpretation. It provides the following functionality to assist users in visualizing, measuring and documenting measurements: Feature segmentation; Feature measurement; and Exportation of measurement results in DICOM Structured Report and a downloadable .docx file for users to review and to use full or partial list of software-generated measurements to prepare their own radiology report.
target_condition: Lumbar spine pathology requiring measurement and quantification (e.g., stenosis, disc herniation, vertebral slippage, spondylolisthesis)
ground_truth: For segmentation: per-pixel majority opinion of 5 independent U.S. radiologists using specialized pixel labeling tool (≥3 of 5 radiologists required for pixel inclusion). For measurements: mean of 5 radiologists' measurements using commercial software tool.
other_metrics: Primary endpoints - Mean Absolute Error (MAE) with 95% confidence intervals for 4 measurements: Dural Sac Area (Axial) 17.2 mm² (limit 20 mm²), Spinal Canal Area (Axial) 23.3 mm² (limit 30 mm²), Lordotic Angle (Sagittal) 3.4° (limit 6°), Vertebral Body Slippage (Sagittal) 0.6 mm (limit 2 mm). Secondary endpoints - Mean Dice Coefficient (MDC) with 95% confidence intervals for 14 anatomical structures (ranging from 0.740 to 0.946, all exceeding MDC limits of 0.7-0.8) and MAE for 10 additional measurements (ranging from 0.81 to 1.74 mm, all within 2 mm limits).
